reason: Reconciliation bill under H. Con. Res. 14 is a legislative vehicle for majoritarian budget changes without filibuster—classic fiscal bleed and entitlement erosion vector. Title II language historically conceals rider payloads targeting Medicaid, SNAP, tax expenditures, or deficit mechanics.
vectors: Fiscal bleed via reconciliation payload (potential debt-limit gimmickry)
Tax expenditure carve-outs protecting specific sectors
Regulatory capture via technical amendments buried in reconciliation text
title: An act to provide for reconciliation pursuant to title II of H. Con. Res. 14.

reason: Medicare Advantage payment acceleration creates fiscal bleed (front-loading CMS outlays) and likely regulatory capture (preferential treatment for MA plans over traditional Medicare). Prompt-pay mechanics typically mask actuarial transfers between program lines.
vectors: Fiscal bleed: accelerated Medicare Advantage payment schedules increase near-term deficit pressure
Regulatory capture: structural preference for private MA plans vs. traditional Medicare
Entitlement erosion: payment acceleration may force traditional Medicare rebalancing
Hidden subsidy: 'prompt pay' language obscures cost-shifting to CMS trust fund
title: Medicare Advantage Prompt Pay Act
